preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced velar fricative (ɣ) to a voiceless uvular fricative (χ) involves both devoicing and a shift in the place of articulation from velar to uvular. This shift is observed in certain dialectal contexts, often influenced by surrounding phonetic environments.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Uvularization, Backing, Regressive Assimilation (specifically devoicing assimilation)

examples.note: In sedentary varieties of Oran Dialectal Arabic, /ɣ/ undergoes regressive devoicing and uvularization to [χ], contrasting with bedouin varieties where /ɣ/ is retained. This often occurs due to assimilation to a following voiceless sound.

sources: Labed, Z. (2015). Genealogical Koineisation in Oran Dialectal Arabic A Case Study. PhD Thesis, University of Oran.
Labed, Z. (2015). Genealogical Koineisation in Oran Dialectal Arabic A Case Study. ResearchGate.
